ElementDescription: The NDAR Global Unique Identifier (GUID) for research subject
Subject ID how it's defined in lab/project
Date on which the interview/genetic test/sampling/imaging/biospecimen was completed. MM/DD/YYYY
Age in months at the time of the interview/test/sampling/imaging.
Notes: Age is rounded to chronological month. If the research participant is 15-days-old at time of interview, the appropriate value would be 0 months. If the participant is 16-days-old, the value would be 1 month.

ElementDescription: Sex of subject at birth
Notes: M = Male; F = Female; O=Other; NR = Not reported

ElementDescription: Life in America is good for me
Notes: 1=Strongly disagree; 2=Disagree; 3=Somewhat disagree; 4=Neither agree nor disagree; 5=Somewhat agree; 6=Agree; 7=Strongly agree

ElementDescription: I think of myself primarily as an American, and seldom as a member of an ethnic or racial group
Notes: 1=Strongly disagree; 2=Disagree; 3=Somewhat disagree; 4=Neither agree nor disagree; 5=Somewhat agree; 6=Agree; 7=Strongly agree

ElementDescription: I think many of the stereotypes about my ethnic/racial group are true
Notes: 1=Strongly disagree; 2=Disagree; 3=Somewhat disagree; 4=Neither agree nor disagree; 5=Somewhat agree; 6=Agree; 7=Strongly agree

ElementDescription: I go through periods when I am down on myself because of my ethnic group membership
Notes: 1=Strongly disagree; 2=Disagree; 3=Somewhat disagree; 4=Neither agree nor disagree; 5=Somewhat agree; 6=Agree; 7=Strongly agree

ElementDescription: It is important for multiculturalists to be connected to people from many different groups, such as Latino/as, Asian Americans, European Americans, Jews, gays and lesbians, Blacks, multiethnic, etc.
Notes: 1=Strongly disagree; 2=Disagree; 3=Somewhat disagree; 4=Neither agree nor disagree; 5=Somewhat agree; 6=Agree; 7=Strongly agree

ElementDescription: I have a strong feeling of hatred and disdain for the majority culture
Notes: 1=Strongly disagree; 2=Disagree; 3=Somewhat disagree; 4=Neither agree nor disagree; 5=Somewhat agree; 6=Agree; 7=Strongly agree

ElementDescription: I am not so much a member of a racial group as I am an American
Notes: 1=Strongly disagree; 2=Disagree; 3=Somewhat disagree; 4=Neither agree nor disagree; 5=Somewhat agree; 6=Agree; 7=Strongly agree

ElementDescription: I believe that only people who accept a perspective from their ethnic/racial group can truly solve the race problem in America
Notes: 1=Strongly disagree; 2=Disagree; 3=Somewhat disagree; 4=Neither agree nor disagree; 5=Somewhat agree; 6=Agree; 7=Strongly agree

ElementDescription: I believe it is important to have a multicultural perspective that is inclusive of everyone
Notes: 1=Strongly disagree; 2=Disagree; 3=Somewhat disagree; 4=Neither agree nor disagree; 5=Somewhat agree; 6=Agree; 7=Strongly agree

ElementDescription: When I look in the mirror, sometimes I do not feel good about the ethnic/racial group I belong to
Notes: 1=Strongly disagree; 2=Disagree; 3=Somewhat disagree; 4=Neither agree nor disagree; 5=Somewhat agree; 6=Agree; 7=Strongly agree

ElementDescription: If I had to put a label on my identity, it would be ''American'' and not a specific ethnic/racial group
Notes: 1=Strongly disagree; 2=Disagree; 3=Somewhat disagree; 4=Neither agree nor disagree; 5=Somewhat agree; 6=Agree; 7=Strongly agree

ElementDescription: When I read the newspaper or a magazine, I always look for articles and stories that deal with race and ethnic issues
Notes: 1=Strongly disagree; 2=Disagree; 3=Somewhat disagree; 4=Neither agree nor disagree; 5=Somewhat agree; 6=Agree; 7=Strongly agree

ElementDescription: When people say things about my group that sound stereotypical, I find myself agreeing with them
Notes: 1=Strongly disagree; 2=Disagree; 3=Somewhat disagree; 4=Neither agree nor disagree; 5=Somewhat agree; 6=Agree; 7=Strongly agree

ElementDescription: We cannot truly be free as a people until our daily lives are guided by values and principles grounded in our ethnic/racial heritage
Notes: 1=Strongly disagree; 2=Disagree; 3=Somewhat disagree; 4=Neither agree nor disagree; 5=Somewhat agree; 6=Agree; 7=Strongly agree

ElementDescription: Members of the dominant group should be destroyed
Notes: 1=Strongly disagree; 2=Disagree; 3=Somewhat disagree; 4=Neither agree nor disagree; 5=Somewhat agree; 6=Agree; 7=Strongly agree

ElementDescription: Privately, I sometimes have negative feelings about being a member of my ethnic/racial group
Notes: 1=Strongly disagree; 2=Disagree; 3=Somewhat disagree; 4=Neither agree nor disagree; 5=Somewhat agree; 6=Agree; 7=Strongly agree

ElementDescription: If I had to put myself into categories, first I would say I am an American, and second I am a member of a racial or ethnic group
Notes: 1=Strongly disagree; 2=Disagree; 3=Somewhat disagree; 4=Neither agree nor disagree; 5=Somewhat agree; 6=Agree; 7=Strongly agree

ElementDescription: People should relax about being too politically correct because some stereotypes about our group are true
Notes: 1=Strongly disagree; 2=Disagree; 3=Somewhat disagree; 4=Neither agree nor disagree; 5=Somewhat agree; 6=Agree; 7=Strongly agree

ElementDescription: When I have a chance to decorate a room, I tend to select pictures, posters or works of art that express strong ethnic-cultural themes
Notes: 1=Strongly disagree; 2=Disagree; 3=Somewhat disagree; 4=Neither agree nor disagree; 5=Somewhat agree; 6=Agree; 7=Strongly agree

ElementDescription: I hate people from the dominant racial/ethnic group
Notes: 1=Strongly disagree; 2=Disagree; 3=Somewhat disagree; 4=Neither agree nor disagree; 5=Somewhat agree; 6=Agree; 7=Strongly agree

ElementDescription: I respect the ideas that other people hold, but I believe that the best way to solve our problems is to think from an ethnic/racial point of view
Notes: 1=Strongly disagree; 2=Disagree; 3=Somewhat disagree; 4=Neither agree nor disagree; 5=Somewhat agree; 6=Agree; 7=Strongly agree

ElementDescription: When I vote in an election, the first thing I think about is the candidate's record on racial and cultural issues
Notes: 1=Strongly disagree; 2=Disagree; 3=Somewhat disagree; 4=Neither agree nor disagree; 5=Somewhat agree; 6=Agree; 7=Strongly agree

ElementDescription: I believe it is important to have both an ethnic identity and a multicultural perspective, because this connects me to other groups (Blacks, Hispanics, Asian Americans, Whites, Jews, gays and lesbians, American Indians, etc.)
Notes: 1=Strongly disagree; 2=Disagree; 3=Somewhat disagree; 4=Neither agree nor disagree; 5=Somewhat agree; 6=Agree; 7=Strongly agree

ElementDescription: During a typical week in my life, I think about ethnic and cultural issues many, many times
Notes: 1=Strongly disagree; 2=Disagree; 3=Somewhat disagree; 4=Neither agree nor disagree; 5=Somewhat agree; 6=Agree; 7=Strongly agree

ElementDescription: We will never be whole until we embrace our ethnic/racial heritage
Notes: 1=Strongly disagree; 2=Disagree; 3=Somewhat disagree; 4=Neither agree nor disagree; 5=Somewhat agree; 6=Agree; 7=Strongly agree

ElementDescription: My negative feelings toward the majority culture are very intense
Notes: 1=Strongly disagree; 2=Disagree; 3=Somewhat disagree; 4=Neither agree nor disagree; 5=Somewhat agree; 6=Agree; 7=Strongly agree

ElementDescription: I sometimes have negative feelings about being a member of my group
Notes: 1=Strongly disagree; 2=Disagree; 3=Somewhat disagree; 4=Neither agree nor disagree; 5=Somewhat agree; 6=Agree; 7=Strongly agree

ElementDescription: As a multiculturalist, it is important for me to be connected with individuals from all cultural backgrounds (gays and lesbians, African Americans, Jews, Native Americans, Asian Americans, Latinos, etc.)
Notes: 1=Strongly disagree; 2=Disagree; 3=Somewhat disagree; 4=Neither agree nor disagree; 5=Somewhat agree; 6=Agree; 7=Strongly agree

ElementDescription: My ethnic/racial group shares characteristics that are reflected in the stereotypes about us.
Notes: 1=Strongly disagree; 2=Disagree; 3=Somewhat disagree; 4=Neither agree nor disagree; 5=Somewhat agree; 6=Agree; 7=Strongly agree

ElementDescription: Cross Ethnic-Racial Identity Scale-Adult: Assimilation (AM) mean score
Notes: Mean of items ceris_a2, 7, 11, 17

ElementDescription: Cross Ethnic-Racial Identity Scale-Adult: Miseducation (MD) mean score
Notes: Mean of items ceris_a3, 13, 18, 29

ElementDescription: Cross Ethnic-Racial Identity Scale-Adult: Self-hatred (SH) mean score
Notes: Mean of items ceris_a4, 10, 16, 27

ElementDescription: Cross Ethnic-Racial Identity Scale-Adult: Anti-dominant (AD) mean score
Notes: Mean of items ceris_a6, 15, 20, 26

ElementDescription: Cross Ethnic-Racial Identity Scale-Adult: Ethnocentricity (ET) mean score
Notes: Mean of items ceris_a8, 14, 21, 25

ElementDescription: Cross Ethnic-Racial Identity Scale-Adult: Multiculturalist inclusive (MI) mean score
Notes: Mean of items ceris_a5, 9, 23, 28

ElementDescription: Cross Ethnic-Racial Identity Scale-Adult: Ethnic-racial salience (ERS) mean score
Notes: Mean of items ceris_a12, 19, 22, 24
